article.headline: Dortmund score three goals in 15mins to beat Gladbach 4-2
article.description: Borussia Dortmund scored three goals in 15 minutes in the first half to power back from two goals down and beat Borussia Moenchengladbach 4-2 on Saturday for their first win in the last four Bundesliga matches.
article.linkText: Dortmund stage comeback to defeat Gladbach 4-2

article.story:
Borussia Dortmund scored three goals in 15 minutes in the first half to power back from two goals down and beat Borussia Moenchengladbach 4-2 on Saturday for their first win in the last four Bundesliga matches.

The hosts, who face AC Milan in the Champions League next week, had found themselves 2-0 down after Gladbach's strong start.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Rocco Reitz put the visitors in front in the 13th minute and Manu Kone thundered in his shot in the 28th minute but Dortmund responded almost instantly, with Marcel Sabitzer cutting the deficit two minutes later.

With Gladbach unable to keep possession, the hosts needed just two more minutes to equalise with Niclas Füllkrug's fourth goal of the season.

Jamie Bynoe-Gittens completed their three-goal comeback on the stroke of halftime, finishing off a quick passing move.

Dortmund kept up the pressure after the break with Marco Reus hitting the woodwork and Germany international Füllkrug coming close as well with a short-range header.

They survived some late Gladbach pressure and scored again on the break with Donyell Malen tapping into an empty goal in stoppage time.

The win lifted Dortmund to 24 points in fourth place, 10 points off leaders Bayer Leverkusen.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 0, Borussia Mönchengladbach 1. Rocco Reitz (Borussia Mönchengladbach) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Alassane Pléa with a through ball.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 0, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2. Manu Koné (Borussia Mönchengladbach) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Nico Elvedi with a headed pass   following a corner.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 1, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2. Marcel Sabitzer (Borussia Dortmund) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Julian Brandt   following a fast break.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 2, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2. Niclas Füllkrug (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Jamie Bynoe-Gittens with a headed pass.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 3, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2. Jamie Bynoe-Gittens (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Niclas Füllkrug.
Second Half begins Borussia Dortmund 3, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Giovanni Reyna replaces Marcel Sabitzer.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Tomás Cvancara replaces Jordan Siebatcheu.
Julian Weigl (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Nathan Ngoumou replaces Rocco Reitz.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Donyell Malen replaces Jamie Bynoe-Gittens.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Salih Özcan replaces Emre Can.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Robin Hack replaces Franck Honorat.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Florian Neuhaus replaces Alassane Pléa.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Youssoufa Moukoko replaces Niclas Füllkrug.
Julian Brandt (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Borussia Mönchengladbach. Christoph Kramer replaces Julian Weigl.
Manu Koné (Borussia Mönchengladbach) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Marius Wolf replaces Marco Reus.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 4, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2. Donyell Malen (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal    following a fast break.
Second Half ends, Borussia Dortmund 4, Borussia Mönchengladbach 2.
